factors.summary: Demand is improving in many core markets.

evidence.text: The volume of OEC on rent increased 14.4 percent, including the impact of the Neff acquisition. We believe that the increases in the volume of OEC on rent and rental rates reflect improving demand in many of our core markets.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows a clear improvement in demand with a 14.4% increase in OEC on rent.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: The gross profit increase includes the impact of the Neff acquisition. The gross margin increase primarily reflects increased rental rates partially offset by a 30 basis point decrease in time utilization.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company successfully increased rental rates, contributing to gross margin growth.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: The improvement in the Pump Solutions region reflects growth in revenue from upstream oil and gas customers, which have experienced significant volatility in recent years. Additionally, due in part to the upstream oil and gas volatility, we have sought to diversify our revenue mix to achieve a reduced portion of business tied to oil and gas.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifting revenue mix towards higher-margin segments like upstream oil and gas.

evidence.text: The volume of OEC on rent increased 14.4 percent, including the impact of the Neff acquisition. On a pro forma basis including the standalone, pre-acquisition results of Neff, the volume of OEC on rent increased 5.3 percent.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes growing at 14.4% and 5.3% on a pro forma basis indicate strong demand.

factors.summary: The Power and HVAC regions are growing fastest.

evidence.text: The improvement in the Pump Solutions region reflects growth in revenue from upstream oil and gas customers... The Power and HVAC region experienced growth in revenue from oil and gas, and non-residential construction, customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Power and HVAC regions show significant growth, driven by oil and gas and construction sectors.

evidence.text: The decreases in SG&A expense as a percentage of revenue for the three and six months ended June 30, 2018 primarily reflect i) a reduction in salaries, stock compensation and bonuses as a percentage of revenue and ii) decreased bad debt expense primarily due to improved accounts receivable aging.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in SG&A expenses, particularly salaries and bonuses, indicates cost management.

evidence.text: For the six months ended June 30, 2018, equipment rentals gross margin decreased 40 basis points, primarily reflecting a 60 basis point decrease in time utilization and increased depreciation of rental equipment, partially offset by a 2.4 percent rental rate increase.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross margins are decreasing due to lower time utilization and higher depreciation.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are underway.

evidence.text: The restructuring charges primarily reflect severance and branch closure charges associated with our restructuring programs... The restructuring program also includes actions undertaken associated with Project XL, which is a set of eight specific work streams focused on driving profitable growth through revenue opportunities and generating incremental profitability through cost savings across our business.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring programs and Project XL are underway to reduce costs and improve profitability.

evidence.text: While the volume of OEC on rent increased 19.3 percent and equipment rentals increased 19.5 percent, depreciation of rental equipment increased 23.7 percent due primarily to the NES and Neff acquisitions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's cautious optimism suggests a positive outlook despite challenges.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include challenges from oil and gas volatility and integration of acquisitions.

evidence.text: Additionally, due in part to the upstream oil and gas volatility, we have sought to diversify our revenue mix to achieve a reduced portion of business tied to oil and gas... The decrease in time utilization primarily reflected the impact of the NES and Neff acquisitions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include significant challenges from oil and gas volatility and integration issues.

factors.summary: Commodity prices and volume changes are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: The gross margin decrease primarily reflects a 90 basis point decrease in time utilization and increased depreciation of rental equipment, partially offset by increased rental rates. For the six months ended June 30, 2018 and 2017, time utilization was 68.0 percent and 68.9 percent, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The focus is on key sensitivities without a clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new contracts and market diversification.

evidence.text: The improvement in the Pump Solutions region reflects growth in revenue from upstream oil and gas customers... We have diversified outside of oil and gas, and have grown our revenue from most of our non oil and gas customers (for example, industrial, construction and mining customers).

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New contracts and market diversification indicate positive growth opportunities.
